behaviors.description: Command segmentation treats newline-separated statements as separate segments, not one monolithic block.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 Unit: this is parser logic at one local function/object boundary (MOCK-MISS says the failure is in segmentation itself). Counter-argument up to Integration rejected: behavior text only asks for segmenting newline-separated statements; no module handoff, OS/network/subprocess, or AI output is required. REJECTION-GATE evidence: "separate segments, not one monolithic block" is purely segmentation behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Call the segmentation function with a multiline shell block and assert it returns distinct ordered segments for each newline-separated statement.

behaviors.description: A multi-line shell block with variable assignments followed by `gh pr create` is recognized as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 Integration: real failure appears when segmented statements are handed to trigger classification (module interaction), matching the incident where whole block became `not_a_trigger`. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess required if command text is injected, (b) no AI/ML output, (c) no multi-agent chain, (d) no LLM stage handoff. Counter-argument down to Unit rejected because behavior combines segmentation + trigger detection across components; bug can hide at their boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Run PostToolUse classification flow with real segmenter + trigger matcher on a multiline command containing assignments then `gh pr create`, and assert classification is PR-create trigger (not `not_a_trigger`).

behaviors.description: A similar multi-line block that ends with `gh issue create` is not incorrectly treated as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 Integration: negative-path correctness depends on interaction of segmentation and trigger-specific matching (avoid false positive across modules). INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no real infra dependency, no AI dependency, no agentic sequence. Counter-argument down to Unit rejected because this behavior targets boundary correctness after segmentation, not just isolated pattern matching.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the same PostToolUse parsing/classification pipeline on multiline assignments ending in `gh issue create`; assert it is classified as non-PR trigger.

behaviors.description: When a PR-create trigger is detected, the hook can reconstruct and persist the PR identifier needed by the review loop state.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 Integration: requires wiring trigger detection, PR-id reconstruction, and state persistence together; local filesystem persistence is explicitly in scope for Integration. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no required real subprocess/network stated; behavior is about reconstruct+persist state, (b) no AI output, (c) no multi-agent chain. Counter-argument up to System rejected by behavior text: it asks to "reconstruct and persist" identifier for state, not validate real `gh`/HTTP behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke PostToolUse with a detected PR-create event and fixture data containing PR identifier signals, then assert persisted review-loop state contains the reconstructed PR identifier in expected field(s).

behaviors.description: After PR-create detection, the review gate state is written in a form that the pre-tool enforcement hook can read and enforce.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 Integration: true failure boundary is cross-module contract (writer hook -> persisted state -> reader/enforcer hook). INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no mandatory real OS/network/subprocess beyond local file I/O, no AI dependence, no multi-agent sequence. Counter-argument down to Unit rejected because a single-function test cannot catch schema/serialization compatibility between two hooks.
behaviors.test_description: Run PR-create path to write gate state, then invoke the pre-tool enforcement hook against that persisted state and assert it loads successfully and enforces the gate decision.
